content.text: The ingress-nginx controller is pending because it needs a node with `ingress-ready=true` label. Let me fix that first, then set up the HTTPS ingress and TCP proxy.

content.text: Controller is running. Now I'll create the TLS secret for `crdb-ui.example.com`, the HTTPS Ingress, and the TCP ConfigMap for SQL passthrough.

input.description: Generate self-signed TLS cert for crdb-ui.example.com

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret tls crdb-ui-tls \
  --cert=/tmp/crdb-tls.crt --key=/tmp/crdb-tls.key 2>&1
input.description: Create TLS secret in cockroachdb namespace

content.content: secret/crdb-ui-tls created

tool_use_result.stdout: secret/crdb-ui-tls created

content.text: Now create the HTTPS Ingress for the DB Console (port 8080):

input.content:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: crdb-ui
  namespace: cockroachdb
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: "HTTPS"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-passthrough: "false"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - crdb-ui.example.com
    secretName: crdb-ui-tls
  rules:
  - host: crdb-ui.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: crdb-cluster-public
            port:
              number: 8080

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/crdb-ingress.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_use_result.content:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: crdb-ui
  namespace: cockroachdb
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: "HTTPS"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-passthrough: "false"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - crdb-ui.example.com
    secretName: crdb-ui-tls
  rules:
  - host: crdb-ui.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: crdb-cluster-public
            port:
              number: 8080

content.text:
Now handle TCP port 26257. nginx-ingress uses a ConfigMap (`tcp-services`) to expose TCP traffic. I need to:
1. Create the `tcp-services` ConfigMap
2. Patch the controller deployment to reference it
3. Add port 26257 to the controller Service

input.description: Get ingress-nginx services

content.content:
NAME                                 TYPE        CLUSTER-IP     EXTERNAL-IP   PORT(S)                      AGE
ingress-nginx-controller             NodePort    10.96.223.61   <none>        80:31918/TCP,443:30136/TCP   7m23s
ingress-nginx-controller-admission   ClusterIP   10.96.238.82   <none>        443/TCP                      7m19s

tool_use_result.stdout:
NAME                                 TYPE        CLUSTER-IP     EXTERNAL-IP   PORT(S)                      AGE
ingress-nginx-controller             NodePort    10.96.223.61   <none>        80:31918/TCP,443:30136/TCP   7m23s
ingress-nginx-controller-admission   ClusterIP   10.96.238.82   <none>        443/TCP                      7m19s

input.description: Create tcp-services ConfigMap for SQL port passthrough

content.content: configmap/tcp-services created

tool_use_result.stdout: configmap/tcp-services created

content.text: Now patch the controller deployment to add `--tcp-services-configmap` and expose port 26257 in the container:

input.command:
kubectl -n ingress-nginx get deploy ingress-nginx-controller -o json | \
  python3 -c "
import json, sys
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
c = d['spec']['template']['spec']['containers'][0]

# Add --tcp-services-configmap arg if not already present
args = c.get('args', [])
tcp_flag = '--tcp-services-configmap=\$(POD_NAMESPACE)/tcp-services'
if not any('tcp-services-configmap' in a for a in args):
    args.append('--tcp-services-configmap=\$(POD_NAMESPACE)/tcp-services')
c['args'] = args

# Add port 26257 if not already there
ports = c.get('ports', [])
if not any(p.get('containerPort') == 26257 for p in ports):
    ports.append({'containerPort': 26257, 'hostPort': 26257, 'name': 'sql', 'protocol': 'TCP'})
c['ports'] = ports

print(json.dumps(d))
" | kubectl apply -f - 2>&1
input.description: Patch ingress-nginx controller deployment to add tcp-services-configmap and port 26257

content.content: deployment.apps/ingress-nginx-controller configured

tool_use_result.stdout: deployment.apps/ingress-nginx-controller configured

content.text: Now patch the NodePort Service to expose port 26257:

input.command:
kubectl -n ingress-nginx get svc ingress-nginx-controller -o json | \
  python3 -c "
import json, sys
s = json.load(sys.stdin)
ports = s['spec']['ports']
if not any(p.get('port') == 26257 for p in ports):
    ports.append({'name': 'sql', 'port': 26257, 'targetPort': 26257, 'protocol': 'TCP'})
s['spec']['ports'] = ports
print(json.dumps(s))
" | kubectl apply -f - 2>&1

input.command:
# Get the node IP
NODE_IP=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")].address}')
echo "Node IP: $NODE_IP"

# Test HTTPS ingress (expect CRDB redirect/login page)
curl -sk --resolve crdb-ui.example.com:443:$NODE_IP \
  https://crdb-ui.example.com/ -o /dev/null -w "HTTPS UI status: %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

# Test SQL port TCP connectivity
timeout 5 bash -c "echo '' | nc -w3 $NODE_IP 30929" 2>&1 || true
echo "TCP 26257 nodePort 30929 reachable check done"
